snippets.title: Shallow vs deep MLP in PyTorch (width vs depth)
snippets.code:
import torch.nn as nn

# UAT-style: one wide hidden layer
shallow = nn.Sequential(
    nn.Linear(20, 512),
    nn.ReLU(),
    nn.Linear(512, 1),
)

# Alternative: deeper, narrower stack
deep = nn.Sequential(
    nn.Linear(20, 128),
    nn.ReLU(),
    nn.Linear(128, 64),
    nn.ReLU(),
    nn.Linear(64, 32),
    nn.ReLU(),
    nn.Linear(32, 1),
)

print("shallow params:", sum(p.numel() for p in shallow.parameters()))
print("deep params:", sum(p.numel() for p in deep.parameters()))
snippets.explanation: Both are feedforward MLPs with non-linear activations. Classic UAT concerns the shallow wide form; deep stacks are a practical parameter-efficiency choice — compare validation loss, not theorem alone.

snippets.title: Visualizing bump-like hidden units (1-D intuition)
snippets.code:
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

x = np.linspace(-2, 2, 400)

def relu_bump(x, center, width):
    return np.maximum(0, 1 - np.abs(x - center) / width)

# Sum of localized bumps approximates a wavy target
centers = [-1.0, -0.3, 0.4, 1.2]
weights = [0.8, -1.2, 0.6, 0.9]
approx = sum(w * relu_bump(x, c, 0.5) for w, c in zip(weights, centers))

plt.plot(x, approx)
plt.title("Sum of hidden-unit bumps (UAT intuition)")
plt.show()
snippets.explanation: Each hidden neuron contributes a localized non-linear piece; weighted sums build complex curves — the intuition behind 'enough neurons can approximate continuous f*'.

snippets.title: Train vs validation gap after fitting training data tightly
snippets.code:
# Pseudocode pattern — check generalization UAT does not guarantee
for epoch in range(500):
    train_loss = train_one_epoch(model, train_loader)
    val_loss = evaluate(model, val_loader)
    print(f"epoch {epoch}: train={train_loss:.4f} val={val_loss:.4f}")

# If train_loss -> 0 but val_loss stays high:
# expressive class (UAT) + memorization != generalization
snippets.explanation: Monitor both splits. Low training error confirms the model class can fit; validation error measures what UAT omits — performance on unseen rows.
